schema_note: Single-file adversarially-verified trim map for skills/research/SKILL.md (precedent trims_verified shape, extended per spec R1/R2/R3/R5). See cortex/lifecycle/harness-token-efficiency-trim/evidence.json for the precedent.

riders.R8_exit2_disambiguation: converted — refine/SKILL.md and complete.md inline exit-2 restatements replaced with the lint-safe pointer form 'On exit 2, apply the canonical ambiguous-slug handling in backlog-writeback.md (loaded at lifecycle Step 2)' (matching clarify.md:110). refine's 'Handle failures as in Step 3' back-reference resolves to the surviving generic failure sentence (no double-hop).
riders.R9_index_md_canonicalization: converted (not declined). The conditional altitude criterion was weighed: the registration recipe is read at a phase-end hot path, but (a) the canonical home backlog-writeback.md explicitly states 'Phase references point here rather than restating these bullets', (b) the clarify.md:110 precedent already points phase references there with the '(loaded at lifecycle Step 2)' note, and (c) the recipe is short and re-derivable, so the pointer carries no correctness risk. refine/SKILL.md (research copy + the 'as in Step 4' spec back-reference) and plan.md (plan copy) converted to pointers; each site retains its non-delegable artifact-key literal ('research', 'spec', 'plan'). The back-reference retargets directly to backlog-writeback.md (no pointer-to-pointer).
riders.R10_disjoint_files_race_rule: narrowed (per critical-review finding): kept the plan.md 'Sub-task headings' citation AND the operative rule 'Sub-task siblings that co-schedule in the same batch must have disjoint Files' at implement.md's dispatch-time point-of-use; dropped only the duplicated rationale (shared-worktree race / last-writer-wins / serialize-via-Depends-on), whose full form lives canonically at plan.md.

behavioral_equivalence.q: For tier=complex criticality=high, how many agents dispatch (and by what rule)?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: Determined by the (tier=complex, criticality=high) cell of the fan-out count matrix in fanout.md (cited via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/../lifecycle/references/fanout.md) — NOT inlined in this file. The file states only: floor cell (simple+low)=3, corner cell (complex+critical)=10, both axes raise the count monotonically; the count is an upper bound on investigation breadth, NOT a quota (dispatch fewer if the task offers fewer genuinely distinct angles; do not pad with redundant agents). The literal value for complex+high lives in fanout.md, not here.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: Does NOT state a literal number for complex+high. agent_count is the cell where tier (row) meets criticality (column) in the count matrix at ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/../lifecycle/references/fanout.md — 'the canonical, shared source for the grid.' Only literal cells in this file: floor (simple+low=3), corner (complex+critical=10); both axes raise the count monotonically. The complex+high number comes from fanout.md, not this file.

behavioral_equivalence.q: Which three angles are the mandatory core?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: Codebase, Web, Requirements & Constraints — always dispatched, at every cell.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: Codebase, Web, and Requirements & Constraints (always dispatched, at every cell).

behavioral_equivalence.q: Is the fan-out count matrix inlined here or referenced by pointer?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: Pointer-referenced, not inlined: 'the canonical, shared source for the grid (do not re-inline it here)', cited via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/../lifecycle/references/fanout.md (three such pointers in the file: Step 2 count, Step 3 angle-selection, Step 3 dispatch-protocol).
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: NOT inlined — referenced by a pointer to ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/../lifecycle/references/fanout.md ('the canonical, shared source for the grid'). The file states only two cells (simple+low=3, complex+critical=10) and that both axes raise the count monotonically.

behavioral_equivalence.q: Which angle is dispatched LAST for high/critical, and is it given a summary of the other angles' findings?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: The Adversarial angle is always last for high/critical, dispatched after the core + orchestrator-chosen angles complete. Yes — it is given a summary: 'inject the summarized findings of the completed angles before dispatch'; the dispatch protocol says 'summarize each angle's findings into a brief paragraph and dispatch the adversarial agent with that summary injected'. The prompt body contains a {summarized_findings_from_other_agents} placeholder.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: The Adversarial angle is dispatched LAST for high/critical. Yes, given a summary: 'dispatched after the core + chosen angles complete, over a brief summary of their findings'; dispatch protocol says 'summarize each angle's findings and dispatch the adversarial agent with that summary injected' ({summarized_findings_from_other_agents} placeholder).

behavioral_equivalence.q: Which angles receive the considerations-injection and which are excluded?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: Injected into the mandatory CORE angles only — Codebase, Web, Requirements & Constraints. Excluded: Tradeoffs (so its orthogonal-dimension evaluation is not narrowed by external considerations), Adversarial (it operates on summarized findings of the other angles, not directly on the considerations), and any other orchestrator-chosen angle.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: Injected into the mandatory core angles ONLY — Codebase, Web, Requirements & Constraints. EXCLUDED: Tradeoffs (orthogonal-dimension eval not narrowed), Adversarial (operates on summarized findings, not the considerations directly), and any other orchestrator-chosen angle.

behavioral_equivalence.q: What is the one fixed-contract output heading?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: ## Open Questions — the only fixed contract heading, machine-parsed by cortex-complexity-escalator (cortex_command/lifecycle/complexity_escalator.py). Every other ## section is angle-driven and not parsed by name; its heading and semantics must be preserved exactly.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: ## Open Questions — machine-parsed by cortex-complexity-escalator (cortex_command/lifecycle/complexity_escalator.py); heading and semantics preserved exactly. Otherwise the schema is angle-driven with no fixed heading roster.

behavioral_equivalence.q: What distinguishes lifecycle-mode from standalone-mode output routing?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: Mode is determined by lifecycle-slug presence in $ARGUMENTS (do NOT use directory existence checks). Lifecycle mode (slug present): create cortex/lifecycle/{slug}/ if absent, write synthesis to research.md, emit ## Considerations Addressed if research-considerations non-empty, announce the written path. Standalone mode (slug absent/empty): present synthesis in conversation only, write no file, create no directory, and do NOT emit ## Considerations Addressed.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: Mode determined by lifecycle-slug presence in $ARGUMENTS (explicit rule: use argument presence, NOT directory existence checks). Lifecycle mode (slug present): create cortex/lifecycle/{slug}/ if missing, write synthesis to research.md, announce completion. Standalone mode (slug absent/empty): present synthesis in conversation, write no file, create no directory.

behavioral_equivalence.q: Into which agent prompts is INJECTION_RESISTANCE_INSTRUCTION substituted, and what is the stated substitution scope?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: The named fragment is 'referenced by every agent-prompt code-block below'; when constructing an Agent dispatch, substitute the {INJECTION_RESISTANCE_INSTRUCTION} placeholder with the verbatim canonical text. The placeholder appears in every per-angle prompt block (Codebase, Web, Requirements & Constraints, Tradeoffs, Adversarial), and the 'when composing a different chosen angle' recipe instructs appending {INJECTION_RESISTANCE_INSTRUCTION}. Stated scope: every agent-prompt code-block.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: Substituted into EVERY agent-prompt code-block: 'The following named fragment is referenced by every agent-prompt code-block below. When constructing an Agent tool dispatch, substitute the placeholder {INJECTION_RESISTANCE_INSTRUCTION} with the verbatim canonical text.' Appears in Codebase, Web, Requirements & Constraints, Tradeoffs, Adversarial templates; chosen-angle composition shape instructs appending {INJECTION_RESISTANCE_INSTRUCTION}.

behavioral_equivalence.q: Is the core wave dispatched as one batch in a single response with read-only (no-worktree) agents, and is the second wave present only when an adversarial angle is chosen?
behavioral_equivalence.baseline: Yes. Core wave: 'Dispatch the mandatory core plus the orchestrator-chosen angles ... in parallel, as one batch of Agent tool calls in a single response. No isolation: "worktree"; agents are read-only.' Second wave (Adversarial) is dispatched last for high/critical; 'At low/medium criticality where no adversarial angle was chosen, the core wave is the whole dispatch and there is no second wave.' So the second wave exists only when an adversarial angle is chosen.
behavioral_equivalence.post_trim: Yes — core wave (every angle except the always-last adversarial one) dispatched 'in one batch of Agent calls in a single response.' Agents read-only and non-worktree: 'No isolation: "worktree"; agents are read-only.' Second (adversarial) wave for high/critical after core returns; it does NOT happen at low/medium where no adversarial angle was chosen ('the core wave is the whole dispatch — no second wave.').

Duplication axis ~zero cross-file (precedent finding 6j); yield is condensation of orchestrator-read narration that restates rules whose authority lives in fanout.md, redundant intra-file examples, maintainer asides, and the orchestrator-read output-structure template brackets. Preserved verbatim: all five fenced prompt bodies, every ## Step heading, the ## Open Questions contract heading, the three fanout.md pointers (with ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/ prefix), the always-last adversarial ordering (textual + positional), INJECTION_RESISTANCE_INSTRUCTION text + placeholders + substitution-scope phrase, the considerations h3/placement rule, the Step-1 directory-check negative + parsing rule, and the Step-4 warning-string literals.
